prompt: WRITE A SPEC ONLY — do NOT build, implement, or modify any tool code. This is a requirements→spec task. STEPS: (1) Read the detailed, self-contained requirement: backlog/2026-06-08-mocha-lab-requirement.md (236 lines — why/prototype-reuse/deltas/functional-reqs/validated-design-findings/NFRs/the-two-seams/environment-facts/open-PO-decisions/DoD). (2) Read the working prototype READ-ONLY at tools/mocha-census/ (shoot.py screenshot, extract.py, combine.py, check-health.py, scan.sh, run-full.sh, out/, README.md) to ground the spec in what already EXISTS — reuse, don't reinvent; map what's reused vs net-new. (3) INVOKE the spec-writer skill (Skill tool: appydave:spec-writer) to transform the requirement into a clear, structured, decision-useful, TESTABLE spec for 'Mocha Lab' — a Watchtower visual tool that screenshots Mochaccino designs, lets David rate them (love/good/average + free-text label; keyboard A/S/D + arrows), and feeds verdicts back into the design spec so design quality keeps improving. PRIORITY capabilities to foreground: (a) BENCH MODE — rate N variants of one idea side-by-side; (b) EXPORT-TO-FILE — kill the copy-paste seam. The spec MUST: map prototype reuse (exists → reused vs new deltas); be testable (functional reqs / acceptance criteria); embed the validated design findings the tool maintains (requirement §5); surface the open PO decisions (don't bury — §9); be BUILD-TECHNIQUE-AGNOSTIC (Ralphy OR a Swagger could build it LATER — do NOT assume, recommend, or start a build; building is a separate later decision). (4) WRITE the spec to backlog/2026-06-08-mocha-lab-spec.md. Tag inference/unknown where you extrapolate beyond the requirement. (5) Report ONE line: where the spec landed + headline (X functional reqs, Y open PO decisions). Then complete run-next-workflow bookkeeping (run record in runs/ + move to done/ + reports/ handback — the return-leg has been getting botched; DO NOT skip it).
